violations.description: General employee practices. — --Food employee observed drinking/using open beverage container outside of designated area. Employee uncovered beverage stored on shelf above pizza cooler prep station. Store below and away from work surfaces/stations. Store personal beverage in closed containers that reduce hand to mouth contact.

Preventing contamination of packaged and unpackaged food. — --Raw animal food was not properly separated from raw or cooked ready-to-eat food. Raw beef is stored above ready to eat items in 3 door reach in cooler on North end of cook line.

Preventing contamination from equipment, utensils, and wiping cloths. — --In-use utensil was not properly stored during pauses in food preparation or dispensing. Open ice buckets and ice scoop stored on top of ice machine under dirty HVAC vents. Store protected from possible contamination.

Date marking and disposition of ready-to-eat potentially hazardous food. — --Refrigerated, ready-to-eat potentially hazardous food (prepared more than 24 hours ago) was not properly date marked Establishment currently vacuum sealing raw and cooked meats and applying 30 day shelf life.--Refrigerated, ready-to-eat potentially hazardous food was not consumed/discarded within 10 calendar days from the date of preparation. Establishment currently vacuum sealing raw and cooked meats and applying 30 day shelf life.

Variance requirement for specialized processing methods. — --Food establishment did not obtain approved variance or HACCP plan from regulatory authority for special process of vacuum sealing raw and cooked meats.
General equipment and utensils - Maintenance and operation. — --Nonfood contact equipment/utensil was not maintained in a good repair or sound condition. Counters/shelving are in need of cleaning.
Cleaning frequency of equipment and utensils. — --Food contact surface was not cleaned as frequently as required. Line cooking equipment and refrigeration units have build-up and need cleaning.

Cleaning and maintenance of floors, walls and ceilings. — --Physical facilities (e.g. floors, walls, ceiling etc…) were not clean or not cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. Floors have a build up of grease and food debris and need cleaning.

General food protection. — --Potentially hazardous food was not stored at proper temperature of 41 degrees F or below. Coleslaw, tomatoes, cheese, & lettuce stored at 45-60 degrees F. Single door reach in cooler not maintained at proper temperature.

Preventing contamination of packaged and unpackaged food. — --Different species of raw animal food was not properly separated from each other or arranged according to minimum cooking temperatures. Raw chicken stored next to eggs and raw steaks and above cooked chicken in reach in cooler.--Raw animal food was not properly separated from raw or cooked ready-to-eat food. Raw chicken stored next to coleslaw above butter in reach in cooler on cook line.

Preventing contamination by consumers. — --Food on display for self service was not properly protected by food guards, display cases, or similarly effective means. Customer salad bar not provided with sneeze protection.
Cleaning frequency of equipment and utensils. — --Food contact surface was not cleaned as frequently as required. Microwave, reach in coolers, and reach in freezers need cleaning.

Cleaning and maintenance of floors, walls and ceilings. — --Physical facilities (e.g. floors, walls, ceiling etc…)were not clean or not cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. Floors need cleaning under line cooking equipment, coolers, freezers, and in dish area. Build-up of food debris and grease under equipment.
